title: Demand and supply
summary: 9609 AS — demand and supply shifts, market equilibrium, and marketing's role in shifting demand.
learningObjectives: Explain factors shifting demand and supply.
Interpret surplus and shortage in markets.
Analyse effect of price changes on quantity demanded.
Evaluate how marketing shifts the demand curve.

simpleExplanation.title: Buyers and sellers in balance
simpleExplanation.summary: Demand shows how much customers will buy at each price; supply shows how much firms will sell. Where they meet is equilibrium — marketing can increase demand at every price level.
steps: Demand shifts: income, tastes, substitutes, advertising.
Supply shifts: costs, technology, taxes, number of firms.
Surplus → price falls; shortage → price rises.

flashcards.back: As price falls, quantity demanded rises (ceteris paribus).

flashcards.front: Demand shift vs movement along curve?
flashcards.back: Shift = non-price factor changes; movement = price change only.
Higher income, favourable tastes, rise in substitute price, effective advertising.
Lower costs, better technology, more producers, subsidies.
Quantity supplied > quantity demanded at current price → downward pressure on price.
Quantity demanded > quantity supplied → upward pressure on price.
Advertising and branding can increase demand at each price — demand curve shifts right.
Elasticity measures responsiveness of demand to price changes.

sections.content: **Demand and supply** (3.1.2) gives the **economic foundation** for pricing and marketing strategy. You need basic **curve shifts** for AS — full diagrams optional but **surplus/shortage** language is essential.

items: **Demand increases** (shifts right): higher consumer income, successful advertising, fashion trend, substitute price rise.
**Demand decreases** (shifts left): recession, bad publicity, cheaper substitute launched.
**Supply increases** (shifts right): lower raw material costs, improved technology.
**Supply decreases** (shifts left): higher taxes, supply chain disruption.

sections.question: A viral social media trend doubles interest in a fitness app's product. At the existing price there are waiting lists. Explain using demand/supply language.
sections.solution:
**Demand has shifted right** — taste/awareness increased at every price (marketing/viral promotion effect).

At the **original price**, **quantity demanded exceeds quantity supplied** → **shortage**.

Firm response: **raise price** (skimming) and/or **increase supply** (hire trainers, expand server capacity). Shortage signals **unmet demand** — opportunity if supply can scale.

sections.content: Do not confuse **increase in demand** with **increase in quantity demanded** — the latter is caused only by a **price fall**.

sections.label: Mark a demand and supply question
